For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what limitation is documented for Guidelines?
target_value: For Guidelines, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: - An LLDP must not contain more than one EEE TLV. - LLDP may or may not be enabled on the interfaces supporting EEE. If LLDP is not enabled, it might not be in the optimal operational mode. - EEE TLV should not be exchanged until it negotiates from L1 and it detects peer EEE capabilities. - EEE TLV is disabled by default and enabled only when EEE is active. - EEE and EEE TLV exchange can be enabled or disabled at the interface level.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, how do you configure Configuring the LLDP agent?
target_value: To configure the LLDP agent, follow the documented procedure: 1. By default, the LLDP agent transmits and receive on all interfaces. To customize LLDP behavior on 2. By default, the LLDP agent sets the management address in all TLVs in the following order: 3. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. To customize the list, use the command 4. By default, support for the LLDP-MED TLV is enabled. To customize settings, use the commands. Commands shown in the procedure include: clear lldp neighbors; clear lldp neighbors.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what is ERPS?
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what limitation is documented for Open Shortest Path First version 2 (OSPFv2)?
target_value: Open Shortest Path First version 2 (OSPFv2): OSPFv2 is not supported on the 6000 and 6100 Switch Series. OSPFv2 is not supported on the 4100i Switch Series. Open Shortest Path First version 2 (OSPFv2) is a routing protocol described in RFC2328. It is a Link State- based IGP (Interior Gateway Protocol) routing protocol applied to routers grouped into OSPF areas identified by the routing configuration on each routing switch. It is widely used in medium to large-sized enterprise networks.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, how do you configure Configuring OSPF on the routing switch?

slots.feature: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

target_value: To configure OSPF on the routing switch, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the OSPF instance and enter the OSPF router configuration context using the following 2. Configure a global router ID using the following command. For command details, see router-id 3. Optionally, if the OSPF process was disabled (it is enabled by default), enable the OSPF process 4. Set an OSPF network for the area using the following command. For command details, see ip ospf 5. Optionally, you can disable OSPF on the interface using the following command. For command. Commands shown in the procedure include: router ospf <PROCESS-ID>; router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>; ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>; ip ospf shutdown.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, how do you configure Configuring external route redistribution and control?

slots.feature: Configuring external route redistribution and control

target_value: To configure external route redistribution and control, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable route redistribution using the redistribute command 2. Optionally, modify the default metric for redistribution using the default-metric command.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what limitation is documented for Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3)?
target_value: Open Shortest Path First version 3 (OSPFv3): OSPFv2 is not supported on the 6000 and 6100 Switch Series. OSPFv2 is not supported on the 4100i Switch Series. OSPFv3 is the IPv6 implementation of Open Shortest Path First protocol (OSPFv2 is the IPv4 implementation of this protocol). OSPFv3 is a routing protocol which is described in RFC5340 entitled OSPF for IPv6. It is a link-state based IGP (Interior Gateway Protocol) routing protocol. It is widely used with medium to large-sized enterprise networks.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what does the VLAN interface (except virtual command do?
For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what limitation is documented for Equal Cost Multipath (ECMP)?
target_value: Equal Cost Multipath (ECMP): ECMP is not supported on the HPE Aruba Networking 4100i Switch Series. ECMP is not supported on the HPE Aruba Networking 6000 and 6100 Switch Series. Equal Cost Multipath (ECMP) allows a router to load balance traffic destined to some network that is reachable through multiple equal cost route nexthops. ECMP functionality is dependent on L3 routing being enabled on the router.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, how do you configure Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP)?
target_value: Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP): VRRP is not supported on the HPE Aruba Networking 4100i Switch Series. VRRP is not supported on the HPE Aruba Networking 6000 and 6100 Switch Series. This section describes how to configure and verify VRRP configuration and operational states. - All VRRP configurations work in VRRP context. - VRRP can be configured on physical ports, VLAN interfaces, and LAG interfaces. - VRRP mandates the associated interface to be routing interface.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what limitation is documented for Routing Information Protocol (RIP)?
target_value: Routing Information Protocol (RIP): Routing Information Protocol (RIP, RIP v2 and RIPng) is not supported on the HPE Aruba Networking 6000 and 6100 Switch Series. Routing Information protocol (RIP, RIP v2, RIPng) is a distance-vector routing protocol that uses hop count as a routing metric. It is useful in small networks. RIP can be configured on L3 ports, LAG, VLAN and Loopback interfaces. All configurations work in the interface context and require the associated interface to be routing interface. - RIP/RIP v2: IPv4 implementation of Routing Information protocol defined in RFC 2453. - RIPng: IPv6 implementation of Routing Information protocol defined in RFC 2080.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what SNMP MIB information is documented for Guidelines and limitations?
target_value: For Guidelines and limitations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: - For faster convergence of routed traffic over Smartlink ports, ip neighbor-flood must be enabled on respective SVI interfaces. - Smartlink uses ERPS copp class for flush packets. - The Aruba 6000 and 6100 Switch Series use separate Smartlink coPP policies as ERPS is not supported in matrix platforms. Limitations: - VSX, ISL and MCLAGs cannot be included in Smartlink groups. - Switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled exclude Smartlink ports from STP. - On switches with both Smartlink and STP enabled, loops involving Smartlink and STP are not detected. - On switches with both Smartlink and ERPS enabled, loops involving Smartlink and ERPS are not detected. - ERPS and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port. - Dynamic VLANs (MVRP) and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port. - Loop Protect and Smartlink cannot be enabled on the same port. - Multicast fast convergence is not supported. - Uplink Failure Detection (UFD) is not supported. - MIB and WebUI are not supported.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what is Configuration attributes?
target_value: Configuration attributes represent user-owned data. Although an attribute must be in the configuration category to be modified by a user, not all attributes in the configuration category can be modified after the resource instance is created. Configuration attributes that cannot be changed after the resource is created are called immutable attributes. This distinction matters when using a PUT request, because immutable attributes cannot be included in the request body.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what does the guide say about Enabling Access to the REST API?

slots.topic: Enabling Access to the REST API

target_value: The REST API reference documents POST /rest/v10.xx/login? username=admin. Details: The AOS-CX Web UI and AOS-CX real-time notifications subsystem rely on the REST API, therefore, all three are enabled or disabled together.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, how do you configure Accessing the REST API using the AOS-CX REST API Reference?

slots.feature: Accessing the REST API using the AOS-CX REST API Reference

target_value: To accessing the REST API using the AOS-CX REST API Reference, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log in to the switch using the Login resource: 2. Expand the Login section 3. Expand the resource by clicking POST or the resource name, /login 4. Click Try it out 5. Enter your user name in the User name field 6. Enter your password in the Password field. Commands shown in the procedure include: Logging in and logging out using the AOS-CX REST API Reference.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what is VLAN interfaces?
target_value: VLAN interfaces: - VLAN interfaces are of type vlan. - You can use the DELETE method to delete a VLAN interface. input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what is AnyCLI?
target_value: AnyCLI: is a custom REST resource that allows for a predefined list of troubleshooting CLI commands to be executed via REST API. Only one command is permitted per request and no concurrent requests are allowed. The resource also provides an endpoint to retrieve the full list of available commands. AnyCLI does not support CLI configuration commands. Only a subset of read-only show commands are available. URI - The URI to execute a CLI command: /rest/{version}/cli - The URI to retrieve allowed CLI commands: /rest/{version}/cli/commands Limitations - Valid versions: v10.04 and higher - Invalid version: v1.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, what is Secure Mode?
target_value: Secure Mode: provides a method of setting the security mode of the device. A zeroization is required before switching between enhanced and standard secure modes. Setting secure mode requires populating writable attribute SYSTEM: : secure_mode with the following value: true Limitations - Valid versions: v10.04 and higher - Invalid version: v1.

input_text: For 4100i AOS-CX 10.15, how do you troubleshoot Troubleshooting?
target_value: To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Ensure that you save the session cookie returned from the login request 2. Ensure that you pass the same cookie back to the switch with every REST API request, including 3. Log out from one of the existing sessions 4. Repeat the command and send the correct session cookie or modify the script to send the correct. Commands shown in the procedure include: REST | API response | codes; REST API through a browser because the browser automatically handles the session cookie.; REST sessions and Web UI sessions.; REST API or Web UI result in an HTTP 404 "Page not found" error..
